title: Getting Started with fireR
text: fireR provides two functions Monitoring Trends Burn Severity (MTBS) wildfire perimeter data: get_mtbs() downloads ZIP archive, read_mtbs() reads filters ready--analyse object.
code: library(fireR)

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: output = "sf", read_mtbs() returns sf object containing every fire perimeter MTBS composite dataset — years, states. Plot burn area acreage get quick sense data:
code: fires <- read_mtbs(output = "sf") fires #> Simple feature collection with 31,386 features and 22 fields #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: -178.3 ymin: 17.9 xmax: -65.3 ymax: 71.4 #> CRS:           NAD83 / Conus Albers (EPSG:5070) plot(fires["BurnBndAc"], main = "MTBS Fire Perimeters — All Years")

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Pass single integer keep fires started calendar year:
code: fires_2020 <- read_mtbs(years = 2020, output = "sf") nrow(fires_2020) #> [1] 246

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Supply two-element vector keep fires within inclusive span: Quickly visualise major fires last decade fell:
code: fires_recent <- read_mtbs(years = c(2018, 2023), output = "sf") nrow(fires_recent) #> [1] 1 328 library(sf)  plot(st_geometry(fires_recent), col = "#E25822AA", border = NA,      main = "MTBS Fire Perimeters 2018–2023")

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Set output = "vect" ("terra") receive terra::SpatVector instead sf object — handy rest workflow uses terra:
code: library(terra)  fires_vect <- read_mtbs(years = c(2020, 2023), output = "vect") class(fires_vect) #> [1] "SpatVector" #> attr(,"package") #> [1] "terra"  plot(fires_vect, "BurnBndAc", main = "Recent Fire Perimeters (terra)")

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Set geometry = FALSE drop geometry get plain data.frame. Useful need metadata (fire name, year, acreage, etc.):
code: tbl <- read_mtbs(geometry = FALSE) head(tbl[, c("Incid_Name", "Ig_Date", "BurnBndAc", "Incid_Type")]) #>     Incid_Name   Ig_Date BurnBndAc Incid_Type #> 1  LOWDEN FIRE 1984-07-01   4537.35   Wildfire #> 2  EUREKA FIRE 1984-08-15  11202.06   Wildfire #> ...

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: MTBS ZIP archive ~100 MB. Download get_mtbs(), read disk read_mtbs() every subsequent call.

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Pass cache = TRUE read_mtbs() look ZIP tools::R_user_dir("fireR", "cache") — platform-appropriate user directory persists R sessions:
code: # Download once to the user cache directory get_mtbs(directory = tools::R_user_dir("fireR", "cache"))  # Read from cache on every subsequent call fires <- read_mtbs(cache = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Supply directory path functions control file stored:
code: get_mtbs(directory = "~/data/mtbs_cache") fires <- read_mtbs(cache = "~/data/mtbs_cache")

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: USGS releases updated dataset, use overwrite = TRUE get_mtbs() bypass cache re-download:
code: get_mtbs(directory = tools::R_user_dir("fireR", "cache"), overwrite = TRUE) fires <- read_mtbs(cache = TRUE)

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Progress messages printed default. Suppress verbose = FALSE:
code: fires <- read_mtbs(years = 2022, verbose = FALSE)

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: sf object, full sf dplyr ecosystem available .

title: Getting Started with fireR
code: library(dplyr)  fires_2000s <- read_mtbs(years = c(2000, 2023), output = "sf")  top10 <- fires_2000s |>   slice_max(BurnBndAc, n = 10) |>   select(Incid_Name, Ig_Date, BurnBndAc) |>   st_drop_geometry()  top10

title: Getting Started with fireR
code: library(ggplot2)  fires_all <- read_mtbs(output = "sf")  fires_all |>   st_drop_geometry() |>   mutate(year = as.integer(substr(Ig_Date, 1, 4))) |>   count(year) |>   ggplot(aes(year, n)) +   geom_col(fill = "#E25822", colour = "#8B1A1A", linewidth = 0.3) +   labs(     title = "Number of MTBS Wildfire Perimeters by Year",     x     = "Year",     y     = "Number of fires"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +   theme(     plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),     panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()   )

title: Getting Started with fireR
text: Monitoring Trends Burn Severity programme joint USGS / USFS initiative maps location, extent, burn severity large wildfires (>1 000 acres western US; >500 acres eastern US) across conterminous USA, Alaska, Hawaii, Puerto Rico 1984 present. information: https://www.mtbs.gov/

what: fireR • MTBS Wildfire Perimeters for R
title: fireR • MTBS Wildfire Perimeters for R
text: Fast access MTBS (Monitoring Trends Burn Severity) fire perimeter data straight USGS.

title: fireR • MTBS Wildfire Perimeters for R
text: fireR splits MTBS access two functions: get_mtbs() downloads MTBS ZIP directory read_mtbs() reads, filters, returns data ZIP sf, terra::SpatVector, data.frame Key features: ⚡ Fast downloads — uses curl’s HTTP/2 persistent connection 🗓️ Year filtering — years = c(1986, 2020) keeps fires within span 🗺️ Flexible output — sf, terra, plain data.frame 💾 Optional caching — skip download repeat calls

title: fireR • MTBS Wildfire Perimeters for R
text: development, install :
code: # Install from GitHub (once published) # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("noahweidig/fireR") devtools::install()    # run from the package root

title: fireR • MTBS Wildfire Perimeters for R
code: library(fireR)  fires <- read_mtbs(output = "sf") plot(fires["BurnBndAc"])

title: fireR • MTBS Wildfire Perimeters for R
code: fires_recent <- read_mtbs(years = c(2010, 2023), output = "sf")

title: fireR • MTBS Wildfire Perimeters for R
code: fires_2020 <- read_mtbs(years = 2020, output = "sf")

title: fireR • MTBS Wildfire Perimeters for R
code: fires_vect <- read_mtbs(years = c(2015, 2023), output = "vect")

title: fireR • MTBS Wildfire Perimeters for R
code: tbl <- read_mtbs(geometry = FALSE)

what: Cache the download for future sessions
title: fireR • MTBS Wildfire Perimeters for R
code: # Cache in the default user directory fires <- read_mtbs(cache = TRUE)  # Or supply your own cache path fires <- read_mtbs(cache = "~/data/mtbs_cache")

title: fireR • MTBS Wildfire Perimeters for R
text: Download data disk first get_mtbs():
code: mtbs_dir <- get_mtbs()

title: fireR • MTBS Wildfire Perimeters for R
text: Monitoring Trends Burn Severity (MTBS) multi-agency programme (USGS, USFS) maps location, extent, burn severity large wildfires across conterminous USA, Alaska, Hawaii, Puerto Rico 1984 present.

what: fireR: Fast Access to MTBS Fire Perimeter Data — fireR-package
title: fireR: Fast Access to MTBS Fire Perimeter Data — fireR-package
text: fireR provides two ergonomic entry points MTBS (Monitoring Trends Burn Severity) composite burned-area extent data: get_mtbs() downloading, read_mtbs() reading filtering data sf object, terra::SpatVector, plain data.frame.

title: fireR: Fast Access to MTBS Fire Perimeter Data — fireR-package
text: get_mtbs() — download MTBS fire perimeter ZIP data. read_mtbs() — read, filter, return MTBS fire perimeters.

title: fireR: Fast Access to MTBS Fire Perimeter Data — fireR-package
text: Maintainer: Noah Weidig noah.weidig@ufl.edu

title: Download MTBS perimeter data — get_mtbs
text: Downloads MTBS composite burned-area extent ZIP archive directory. ZIP already exists overwrite = FALSE, network call made.

title: Download MTBS perimeter data — get_mtbs
code: get_mtbs(   directory = getwd(),   overwrite = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download MTBS perimeter data — get_mtbs
text: directory character(1) directory mtbs_perimeter_data.zip stored. Defaults current working directory. overwrite logical(1) re-download TRUE; defaults FALSE. verbose logical(1) print progress messages.

title: Download MTBS perimeter data — get_mtbs
text: character(1) path downloaded ZIP file (invisibly).

title: Download MTBS perimeter data — get_mtbs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ zip_path <- get_mtbs() } # }

what: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
title: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
text: Reads MTBS fire perimeters local MTBS ZIP downloaded get_mtbs() returns either spatial object plain attribute table.

title: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
code: read_mtbs(   years = NULL,   type = NULL,   geometry = TRUE,   output = c("vect", "sf", "terra"),   cache = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
text: years integer vector length 1 2 specifying year range keep. single year supplied, fires year returned. two years supplied treated c(start_year, end_year) (inclusive). NULL (default) returns years without filtering. type character vector incident types keep, matched Incid_Type column. Valid values "Wildfire", "Prescribed Fire", "Unknown", "Wildland Fire Use". NULL (default) returns incident types. geometry logical(1) TRUE (default) result spatial object (sf terra::SpatVector depending output). FALSE shapefile attributes returned plain data.frame geometry column dropped. output character(1) class returned spatial object. Either "vect" / "terra" (default) terra::SpatVector, "sf" sf object. Ignored geometry = FALSE. cache logical(1) character(1). Controls read_mtbs() looks downloaded ZIP file. FALSE (default) current working directory used. TRUE platform user cache directory (tools::R_user_dir("fireR", "cache")) used. Supply directory path string specify custom location. verbose logical(1) print progress messages.

title: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
text: terra::SpatVector output = "vect" / "terra" geometry = TRUE. sf object output = "sf" geometry = TRUE. data.frame geometry = FALSE.

title: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
text: read_mtbs() download data. Use get_mtbs() first obtain ZIP archive.

title: Read MTBS fire perimeter data — read_mtbs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ zip_path <- get_mtbs() fires <- read_mtbs(output = "sf") fires_2020 <- read_mtbs(years = 2020, type = "Wildfire", output = "sf") tbl <- read_mtbs(geometry = FALSE) } # }
